A.does: Drains the fiber's pending update queue, replays skipped base updates, and computes the new derived state for a class component or hook reducer during the render phase.

B.does: Resets all work-in-progress render-loop globals and clones the root fiber into a fresh workInProgress tree to initialize the render context before a new render pass begins.

comparison: processUpdateQueue is called per-fiber inside beginWork to resolve setState/dispatch updates into new state, whereas prepareFreshStack is called once per render attempt at the work-loop level to set up global render state before any fiber processing starts.
notes: processUpdateQueue also has a copy in packages/react-server/src/ReactFizzClassComponent.js for the Fizz SSR path; the reconciler canonical definition is ReactFiberClassUpdateQueue.js:486.
